caption: The video appears to be a presentation slide focusing on an Anti-Litter Campaign, specifically highlighting the issue of cigarette butt littering. The slide features a bold title "Anti-Litter Campaign" at the top, followed by a smaller image and text that reads, "Did you know that cigarette butts are the most commonly littered item?" Below this text is a graphic with the slogan "DON'T TRASH CALIFORNIA," emphasizing the campaign's target region. The slide remains static throughout the frames, suggesting it is part of a larger presentation aimed at raising awareness about the environmental impact of littering, particularly cigarette butts, and encouraging responsible behavior among Californians. The cursor is visible in the upper right corner, indicating that the presentation is being viewed or controlled via a computer screen.
